explainability.findings: Technical papers mention AI agents' transparency logs and their contributions.
explainability.path_to_improvement: Publish user-facing explanation interfaces or documented appeal workflows.

relevant_sources.summary: This technical paper discusses the transformative impact of Generative AI on data analytics, highlighting critical governance challenges. It provides evidence for **explainability** through mentions of AI agents' transparency logs and contributions, **external accountability** by referencing compliance with the EU AI Act and audit trails, **fairness** through discussions of bias mitigation and detection, **governance** via the mention of AI governance, review boards, agent accountability, and AI Foundry, **oversight** through the description of human oversight, **privacy** with the mention of RAG and data protection, and **transparency** by detailing AI agent capabilities, LLMs, and transparency logs.
relevant_sources.title: From complexity to clarity: Generative AI in data analytics

external_accountability.findings: Documentation highlights regulatory compliance in the implementation of their AI system. Technical papers reference compliance with the EU AI Act and audit trails.
external_accountability.path_to_improvement: Publish third-party audit results, certifications, or regulatory compliance documentation.

relevant_sources.summary: This case study on Mosaic's AI-powered credit risk management solution provides evidence for **external accountability**, **governance**, and **transparency**. The document supports external accountability and governance by highlighting regulatory compliance in the implementation of their AI system. Transparency is evidenced through descriptions of the machine learning algorithms used for analysis, the generation of risk scores, and the overall capabilities of the AI system, including future aspirations for its use.
relevant_sources.title: AI-Powered Credit Risk Management at Mosaic

governance.findings: Documentation highlights regulatory compliance in AI system implementation. Technical papers mention AI governance, review boards, agent accountability, and AI Foundry. Articles discuss integrating AI into business processes and leveraging it for efficiency and decision-making, while the privacy policy describes automated data collection and profiling for various purposes. Conference agendas also suggest industry-level engagement with implementing AI models.
governance.path_to_improvement: Name an AI governance body with defined mandate covering all AI use.

Transparency is evidenced through descriptions of the machine learning algorithms used for analysis, the generation of risk scores, and the overall capabilities of the AI system, including future aspirations for its use.

relevant_sources.summary: This third-party article, "Mosaic Builds a Global IT Foundation for Growth," provides evidence for the **governance** and **transparency** pillars of responsible AI. The article implies transparency by mentioning AI features, capabilities, and the implementation of machine learning and generative AI models. Governance is suggested through discussions of integrating AI into business processes and leveraging it for efficiency and decision-making.
relevant_sources.title: Mosaic Builds a Global IT Foundation for Growth

relevant_sources.summary: The Mosaic Company Privacy Policy document provides evidence for the **governance** pillar. It describes automated data collection and profiling for purposes such as tailored advertising, fraud prevention, and credit risk reduction, indicating the presence of automated systems. While these practices are mentioned, the policy is classified as "POLICY" due to a lack of detailed execution information.
relevant_sources.title: Mosaic Company Privacy Policy

relevant_sources.summary: This conference agenda provides evidence for the **governance** and **transparency** pillars of responsible AI. The mention of Paul Jewell from The Mosaic Company discussing "Model Integration to Operations and Front Line Enablement" suggests industry-level engagement with implementing AI models, implying a need for governance over these systems. Furthermore, the agenda's reference to "Guarantees compatible recommendations" points towards mechanisms for ensuring transparency and control over AI outputs.
relevant_sources.title: AGENDA: 2025 Digitalization & AI in Mining North America Conference

transparency.findings: Documentation describes the machine learning algorithms, risk score generation, and overall capabilities of the AI system, including future aspirations. Technical papers reference AI agents' transparency logs, contributions, capabilities, and LLMs. Articles also mention AI features, capabilities, and the implementation of machine learning and generative AI models, while conference agendas reference "Guarantees compatible recommendations" for AI outputs.
transparency.path_to_improvement: Publish detailed documentation (model cards, system specs) for AI systems deployed.

summary.overall_findings: Mosaic's published materials describe the machine learning algorithms used for analysis under the transparency pillar, and discuss bias mitigation within fairness disclosures. Materials also mention AI agents' transparency logs in relation to explainability and highlight regulatory compliance in the implementation of their AI system for governance. All 7 evaluated pillars have documented public evidence, with disclosures addressing policy-level practices across these areas. This assessment draws on 10 publicly available sources.
